LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION. THE COURT TREATED MOTION AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND DENIED AS TO THE GENERAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A VIABLE CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE NO STATUTORY BASIS IS STATED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH DESIGN IMMUNITY DEFENSE. CCSF DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY SHOW THAT THERE WAS DISCRETIONARY APPROVAL OF THE DESIGN. THE BRIDGE DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE PLANS IN THAT IT LACKED THE SPECIFIED MIRRORS AND WARNING LIGHTS/ALARMS. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT NO DANGEROUS CONDITION EXISTED. FACTUAL ISSUE RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF ACTED WITH DUE CARE AND WHETHER HER CONDUCT WAS REASONABLY FORESEEABLE. TRIAL JUDGE TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE DEFENSES ARE TRIED FIRST. PLAINTIFF AGREED TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT 10 DAYS FROM TODAY (09/29/11). PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

COURT SUSTAINS OBJECTIONS TO MOWRER DECLARATION. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE PARTIES DID NOT HAVE A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP. Off Calendar Jan-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jayme L. Burns, Esq., from Gwilliam, Ivay, Chiosso, Cavalli & Brewer, 510-832-5411, for plaintiff and moving party
Scottt B. Cloud, Esq., from Cloud & Olsen, 562-901-1102, for defendant and opposing party ThyssenKrupp Elevator, appearing by telephone
Mark R. Irvine, Esq., from Fitzpatrick & Hunt, Tucker, Collier, Pagano, Aubert, LLP, 213-873-2100, for defendant Cathay Pacific Airways, appearing by telephone
Gwendolyn K. Yee, Esq., from Bennett & Yee, 415-788-6200 for defendant CCSF

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS ELIDA BARILLAS AND MARIO GOMEZ'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Complaint To Add A Plea For Punitive Damages Against Thyssenkrupp Airport Systems is Granted. Plaintiffs have shown good cause for the amendment and defendant ThyssenKrupp has not shown any prejudice that could not be ameliorated by a short trial continuance. regarding the request for punitive damages. Defendant's objections are overruled.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Steven J. Brewer, Esq. of Gwilliam, Ivary, Chiosso, Cavalli & Brewer 510/832-5411 for pltfs & opposing parties; Gwendolyn K. Yee, Esq. of Bennett & Yee 788-6200 for deft & moving party CCSF; Mark R. Irvine, Esq. of Fitzpatrick & Hunt, Tucker, Collier, Pagano, Aubert, LLP 213/873-2131 for x-deft Cathay Pacific Airways Ltd.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling except Court will treat motion as a motion for judgment on the pleadings and deny as to the general negligence cause of action. Plaintiff must plead a viable cause of action because no statutory basis is stated. Remainder of tentative ruling stands - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH DESIGN IMMUNITY DEFENSE. CCSF DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY SHOW THAT THERE WAS DISCRETIONARY APPROVAL OF THE DESIGN. THE BRIDGE DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE PLANS IN THAT IT LACKED THE SPECIFIED MIRRORS AND WARNING LIGHTS/ALARMS. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT NO DANGEROUS CONDITION EXISTED. FACTUAL ISSUE RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF ACTED WITH DUE CARE AND WHETHER HER CONDUCT WAS REASONABLY FORESEEABLE. TRIAL JUDGE TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE DEFENSES ARE TRIED FIRST.
Plaintiff agreed to file the amended complaint 10 days from today (09/29/11). Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
